page.text:
Following the oil spill from the Exxon Valdez the clean-up operation began.
First dispersants were used. The dispersants were dropped from a helicopter using a dispersant bucket. Wave action was, however, slight and the dispersant did not mix with the oil in the water. The use of dispersants was discontinued.
Burning was then attempted using a trial explosion. This was fairly successful but following this, as a result of unfavourable weather conditions, no additional burning was undertaken.
Mechanical clean-up was started shortly afterwards using booms and skimmers, but the operation was delayed because the skimmers were not available during the first 24 hours following the spill.
Prince William Sound contains many rocky coves where the oil collected and therefore the decision was made to displace the oil with high-pressure hot water. However, large numbers of microbial populations on the shoreline were destroyed as a result.
